name: UN Security Council Resolution 1929
description: Fourth round of UN sanctions banning Iranian arms deals, freezing assets of IRGC entities, and restricting travel for designated nuclear officials. Foundation for all subsequent multilateral pressure.
enforcement: All UN member states required to implement. Compliance monitored by UN Panel of Experts.

name: Executive Order 13599 — Iranian Financial Sanctions
target: Government of Iran & Iranian Financial Institutions
description: Froze all property and interests of the Iranian government and financial institutions within US jurisdiction. Blocked the Central Bank of Iran from dollar transactions.
enforcement: OFAC enforcement. Secondary sanctions on foreign banks processing Iranian transactions.
key_provisions: Freeze all Iranian government property in the US
Block Central Bank of Iran (CBI) from SWIFT
Prohibition on USD clearing for Iranian entities

name: Maximum Pressure Campaign
description: Post-JCPOA withdrawal campaign targeting Iran's entire economic ecosystem. Oil exports crashed from 2.5M bpd to under 400K bpd. Collapsed the rial and triggered hyperinflation.
enforcement: OFAC, Treasury, State Dept. Aggressive secondary sanctions enforcement.
key_provisions: Metal sector embargo (steel, aluminum, copper, iron)

name: IRGC Foreign Terrorist Organization Designation
description: First time a branch of a nation's military was designated as a Foreign Terrorist Organization. Criminalizes any material support to IRGC, affecting businesses and governments globally.
enforcement: DOJ criminal prosecution authority. OFAC civil penalties. Visa bans.
key_provisions: Downstream designations of Quds Force networks

name: Houthi Ansar Allah Terrorist Redesignation
description: Redesignated Houthis as Specially Designated Global Terrorist group after Red Sea shipping attacks escalated. Restricts financial flows and weapons procurement networks.
enforcement: OFAC with humanitarian exemptions for Yemen aid operations.

name: JCPOA Snapback — Full UN Sanctions Reimposition
description: Triggered by Iran's confirmed 87% uranium enrichment. All pre-JCPOA UN sanctions snapped back. Russia and China abstained. Conventional arms embargo reimposed.
enforcement: UN Security Council enforcement. All member states required to implement within 30 days.
key_provisions: Asset freeze expansion to new entities

name: Emergency War Powers — Total Economic Embargo
description: Invoked under IEEPA following Iran's ballistic missile strikes on US forces. Total trade embargo. All Iranian assets in the US frozen. All transactions with Iran or Iranian nationals prohibited.
enforcement: Treasury, Commerce, DOJ. Immediate secondary sanctions on any entity conducting business with Iran.
key_provisions: All Iranian assets in US jurisdiction frozen
Secondary sanctions on all trading partners
Travel ban on all Iranian nationals

name: EU Emergency Sanctions Package
description: EU's most comprehensive Iran sanctions package. Full energy import ban, SWIFT disconnection for remaining Iranian banks, asset freezes on 300+ entities. Adopted within 24 hours.
enforcement: EU member state national authorities. Coordinated with US OFAC.
key_provisions: Port access denied to Iranian-flagged vessels

name: GCC Joint Sanctions Framework
description: Unprecedented unified GCC sanctions targeting Iran and all proxy organizations. Severed remaining diplomatic ties. Closed airspace. Frozen $22B in assets across Gulf states.
enforcement: Individual GCC member state enforcement. Saudi-led coordination.
key_provisions: Shipping lane restrictions in Persian Gulf

name: UK Iran Sanctions (War Powers)
description: UK activated emergency sanctions framework concurrent with military operations. London financial center fully closed to Iranian transactions. Proscribed IRGC under UK terrorism legislation.
enforcement: HM Treasury, National Crime Agency, with US and EU coordination.
key_provisions: Lloyd's market insurance ban for Iran

name: Japan & South Korea Coordinated Sanctions
description: Tokyo and Seoul jointly froze Iranian assets and halted all remaining oil trade. Released $6B in previously held Iranian funds to humanitarian-only escrow. Technology export ban.
enforcement: METI (Japan), MOTIE (South Korea). Coordinated with US Treasury.

name: Australia & Canada Wartime Sanctions
description: Five Eyes-aligned sanctions packages. Both nations designated IRGC as terrorist entity, froze assets, and imposed travel bans on 200+ Iranian officials and military commanders.
enforcement: DFAT (Australia), GAC (Canada). Intelligence sharing via Five Eyes.

name: Hezbollah Global Financial Exclusion
description: FATF blacklisted all Hezbollah-linked financial networks. Lebanese banking sector placed under enhanced monitoring. Hawala networks disrupted across 30+ countries.
enforcement: FATF compliance framework. National financial intelligence units in 39 member jurisdictions.
key_provisions: FATF blacklisting of Hezbollah financial networks
Enhanced due diligence on Lebanese banks

name: Russian/Chinese Sanctions Evasion
description: Russia and China refused to implement wartime sanctions. Moscow offered alternative SWIFT routing via SPFS. Beijing maintained oil purchases through yuan-denominated channels. Significant sanctions leakage.
enforcement: None — sanctions evasion. US secondary sanctions imposed on facilitating entities.
key_provisions: SPFS alternative to SWIFT offered to Iran
Diplomatic cover at UN Security Council

name: SWIFT Global Disconnection Order
description: Complete disconnection of all remaining Iranian banks from SWIFT messaging. Even humanitarian trade channels temporarily severed. Iran's international payment capability reduced to zero through legitimate channels.
enforcement: SWIFT compliance team. National bank regulators.
key_provisions: Complete SWIFT disconnection — all 24 Iranian banks

name: Cyber Sanctions & Critical Infrastructure EO
description: Executive order targeting Iranian cyber capabilities after retaliatory attacks disrupted US CENTCOM logistics systems. Sanctions on IRGC Cyber Command, 15 front companies, and 40 individuals. Authorized offensive cyber operations against Iranian infrastructure.
enforcement: NSA, Cyber Command, Treasury OFAC. Coordinated with Five Eyes cyber agencies.
key_provisions: 15 front companies providing cyber tools blacklisted
Authorized US offensive cyber ops against Iranian infrastructure
Technology export ban on dual-use networking equipment
Sanctions on cryptocurrency exchanges facilitating Iranian transactions

name: India Oil Waiver Revocation
description: Revoked India's long-standing sanctions waiver for Iranian oil imports. New Delhi had been importing ~200K bpd through the Chabahar corridor. India complied within 48 hours under threat of secondary sanctions on major Indian banks.
enforcement: OFAC secondary sanctions threat against Indian banking sector.
key_provisions: Indian refiners must certify zero Iranian crude
Payment channels through UCO Bank severed

name: PMF & Militia Financing Network Sanctions
target: Popular Mobilization Forces & Iraqi Militia Networks
description: Joint US-EU designation of 45 PMF-linked entities operating through Iraqi banking system. Targeted Kata'ib Hezbollah and Asa'ib Ahl al-Haq financial networks funneling IRGC funds through Baghdad construction firms.
enforcement: OFAC, EU Council. Iraqi Central Bank notified. Compliance required from Iraqi commercial banks.
key_provisions: IRGC-QF funding channels through Baghdad blocked

name: Strait of Hormuz Maritime Insurance Ban
description: Lloyd's of London and major P&I clubs withdrew all insurance coverage for Iranian vessels and any vessel transiting to Iranian ports. War risk premiums for Strait of Hormuz spiked to 10% of hull value. Global shipping rerouting via Cape of Good Hope.
enforcement: Lloyd's Market Association, International Group of P&I Clubs, EU Insurance Directive.
key_provisions: All P&I coverage for Iranian vessels withdrawn
War risk premiums at 10% hull value for Hormuz transit
Ship-to-ship transfer zones under satellite surveillance
Bunkering services denied in regional ports

name: Iranian Central Bank Gold Reserves Freeze
target: Central Bank of Iran — Gold & Foreign Reserves
description: Coordinated action freezing $18B in Iranian gold reserves held at Swiss, Turkish, and UAE vaults. Switzerland broke neutrality precedent. Remaining $4B in accessible reserves gives Iran approximately 6 weeks of critical imports at wartime consumption rates.
enforcement: Swiss FINMA, US OFAC, ECB. Turkish compliance under heavy pressure.
key_provisions: Swiss vaults freeze $12B in Iranian gold

name: IRGC Navy & Air Force Commander Designations
description: Coordinated designation of 47 IRGC generals and senior commanders personally responsible for missile strikes on US forces. Includes IRGC Navy commander RADM Tangsiri, IRGC-AF commander BG Hajizadeh, and 12 IRGC Ground Forces division commanders. Personal assets, family assets, and associated business holdings frozen globally.
enforcement: OFAC SDN List, EU Restrictive Measures, UK OFSI. Interpol Red Notices requested for 12 commanders.
key_provisions: 47 IRGC generals added to SDN list with full blocking
Family members and known associates included
International arrest warrants via Interpol requested
Business holdings across UAE, Turkey, Iraq seized
Real estate and offshore accounts in 14 countries frozen

name: Emergency Oil Market Stabilization Act
target: Global Oil Market — Iranian Supply Elimination
description: Emergency legislation authorizing SPR release of 1M bpd for 90 days and coordinated IEA reserve release of 2M bpd. Simultaneously imposes 180-day snap-back sanctions on any nation importing Iranian oil. Brent crude dropped from $142 to $128 on announcement.
enforcement: DOE Strategic Petroleum Reserve. IEA 31-nation coordination. OFAC snap-back authority.
key_provisions: IEA coordinated release: 2M bpd from 31 nations
Snap-back sanctions on Iranian oil importers (180-day lookback)
Tanker tracking via satellite — mandatory AIS for all Hormuz transit
Price cap mechanism authorized if Brent exceeds $150

name: Turkish Straits Emergency Transit Order
target: Iranian Naval & Commercial Vessels — Bosphorus & Dardanelles
description: Turkey invoked Montreux Convention wartime provisions to restrict Iranian military vessel transit through Turkish Straits. Extended to commercial vessels carrying sanctioned cargo. Iranian Black Sea trade route effectively severed.
enforcement: Turkish Naval Forces, NATO Maritime Command. Satellite monitoring of all strait transit.
key_provisions: Commercial vessels subject to cargo inspection
Russian-flagged vessels carrying Iranian cargo inspected

name: Cryptocurrency & Digital Asset Emergency Order
description: Emergency G7 order forcing all regulated exchanges to block Iranian-linked wallets. Treasury identified 14,000+ wallets linked to IRGC sanctions evasion network processing ~$2B annually through Bitcoin and Tether. Chainalysis and Elliptic contracted for real-time monitoring.
enforcement: FinCEN, SEC, EU AMLA. G7 Financial Action Task Force emergency session. Exchange compliance within 6 hours.
key_provisions: All G7-regulated exchanges must block within 6 hours
IRGC Bitcoin mining operations in Rafsanjan targeted
Tether/USDT freeze authority invoked on $800M+ in stablecoins

name: Iranian Airspace & Aviation Total Exclusion
target: Iran Air, Mahan Air, All Iranian Carriers & Airspace
description: ICAO issued unprecedented emergency NOTAM declaring all Iranian airspace a conflict exclusion zone. FAA and EASA simultaneously banned all flights to/from/over Iran. Iran Air and Mahan Air grounded globally — 43 aircraft stranded at foreign airports subject to seizure.
enforcement: ICAO Emergency Session, FAA Emergency Order, EASA Safety Directive. National aviation authorities.
key_provisions: Iranian airspace declared conflict exclusion zone
43 Iranian aircraft stranded at foreign airports — seizure orders issued
Iran Air & Mahan Air global operating certificates revoked
Cargo air freight to Iran completely severed
Humanitarian air corridor under UN coordination only

name: Secondary Sanctions on Chinese Oil Refineries
target: Chinese Independent Refineries ('Teapots') Processing Iranian Crude
description: Treasury designated 8 Chinese independent refineries ('teapots') in Shandong province processing Iranian crude. Froze $12B in connected Chinese bank assets accessible to US jurisdiction. Beijing protested as 'economic warfare against China' but refiners began halting Iranian imports within hours.
enforcement: OFAC SDN designations. Correspondent banking restrictions on Bank of Kunlun and 3 regional Chinese banks.
key_provisions: Bank of Kunlun correspondent banking severed
3 regional Chinese banks cut from USD clearing
Beijing retaliatory measures threatened but not yet enacted

name: Iranian Regime Officials Asset Disclosure Order
target: Supreme Leader's Office, Bonyad Mostazafan, IRGC Economic Empire
description: G7 emergency order requiring all member-state financial institutions to disclose holdings linked to Khamenei's office, the Bonyad Mostazafan foundation ($95B estimated), and IRGC's business empire. Discovery phase expected to reveal $40-60B in previously hidden assets across Dubai, Istanbul, and European shell companies.
enforcement: FATF Emergency Plenary. G7 Financial Intelligence Units. 72-hour disclosure deadline for banks.
key_provisions: 72-hour mandatory disclosure by all G7 banks
Supreme Leader's personal financial network mapped
Dubai and Istanbul real estate holdings frozen pending review

name: Humanitarian Corridor Framework
description: Under intense pressure from humanitarian organizations, UN established emergency framework for food and medicine imports to Iran. SWIFT humanitarian channel partially restored after 48-hour blackout. Limited to pre-approved NGOs through single Swiss clearing bank. Criticized as insufficient by MSF and ICRC.
enforcement: UN OCHA coordination. Swiss National Bank humanitarian clearing. OFAC general license.
key_provisions: Single Swiss clearing bank for aid transactions
Food and medicine exempted from total embargo
MSF criticizes 72-hour processing delay as 'death sentence'
Medical device and equipment imports still blocked
